name: Appointment CRUD - Create
description: Customer calls to schedule a new AC repair appointment, providing all required information

prompt.prompt:
You are a homeowner in Atlanta calling Johnson's Air Conditioning & Plumbing to schedule an AC repair. Your AC is making a loud rattling noise and not cooling well. When asked, provide your information one piece at a time:
- Name: David Martinez
- Phone: 404-555-0319
- Email: david.martinez@email.com
- Address: 2845 Roswell Road NE, Atlanta, Georgia
- The rattling started 3 days ago and has gotten louder
- You think it's a central AC unit, about 10 years old
You'd prefer a morning appointment. If offered time slots, pick the earliest morning option. Be cooperative and straightforward. After the appointment is confirmed, thank them and end the call.

extra_evaluation_criteria.name: Availability Checked First
extra_evaluation_criteria.conversation_goal_prompt: The agent called the check_availability tool BEFORE calling schedule_appointment. The agent should always check what times are open before trying to book.

extra_evaluation_criteria.name: All Required Fields Collected
extra_evaluation_criteria.conversation_goal_prompt: The agent collected all required fields before booking: customer name (David Martinez), phone number (404-555-0319), email (david.martinez@email.com), and service address (2845 Roswell Road NE, Atlanta). Each piece should have been collected one at a time.

extra_evaluation_criteria.name: Schedule Tool Called
extra_evaluation_criteria.conversation_goal_prompt: The agent called the schedule_appointment tool to formally book the appointment. The agent should not have just verbally confirmed — the tool must have been invoked.

extra_evaluation_criteria.name: Confirmation Details Relayed
extra_evaluation_criteria.conversation_goal_prompt: After booking, the agent confirmed the appointment details back to the caller, including the time and/or confirmation number. The caller should have been left with a clear understanding of when their appointment is.
